subject: Mathematics
title: Population vs. Sample
description: A fast breakdown of Population vs. Sample for builders.

hook.text: You can't ask every human on Earth their opinion. So you ask a small group and hope it represents everyone.

buildup.text: The population is the entire group you want to study. The sample is the subset you actually measure.

discovery.text: A well-chosen sample can accurately represent millions of people with just a few thousand responses.

twist.text: A badly chosen sample can be wildly wrong. In 1936, a poll predicted the wrong US president from biased data.

climax.text: The key to good statistics is not sample size alone—it's how representatively you select the sample.

punchline.text: A tiny sample can speak for millions—if chosen well.

quiz.question: What is a 'sample' in statistics?
options: A subset of the population selected for study
